TextENGNew: "...How is it that afternoon classes always make you so drowsy?"
Mion's voice, drowsy enough to make whoever heard it yawn along with her, drifted over to me.
"Do you really need a medical explanation for sleepiness? I could give one, but it'll only make you more drowsy."
"...Ugh~ ...Have mercy~..."
Nearly three o'clock.
Sixth period was almost over.
Satoko and the other lower-grade students had been released after fifth period, so they were probably out running around the schoolyard by now.
The cheerful voices of the younger kids drifting up from the yard were genuinely enviable.
"Come on, Mii-chan — you're class rep, you have to set a better example. No falling asleep~."
"You know, I'd rather be captain of the swimming club than class rep. They get to sleep a lot more."
changeReason: Old says 'boxing club' (ボクシング); Japanese says 水泳部 (swimming club). New correctly translates as 'swimming club.'

TextENGNew: "Oh? ...And what's your reasoning?"
"Swimming club — get it? Slee-ming~ club~ ...just kidding~..."
changeReason: Old translates the pun as 'hitting the sack' (boxing); Japanese pun is 水泳/スイミン (swimming/sleeping). New preserves the actual wordplay.

TextENGNew: THWACK!
The teacher cackled as she rapped Mion on the back of the head with the corner of her textbook.
"...And on that note, I think that's all for today. Representative, please close out the class."
"Aaaall riiiise! Attentiooooon!!"
Mion, who had been half-asleep moments before, snapped back to life the instant she heard class was over.
...Watching someone be that shameless about it was almost refreshing.
Homeroom doubled as the final period for the younger grades, so when the last class ended, everyone dispersed on the spot.
changeReason: Old says 'Homeroom was done after the lower grades finished their lessons'; Japanese says homeroom doubles as the final period for lower grades, so when last class ends everyone disperses. New is more accurate to the system described.

TextENGNew: One of the quirks unique to our mixed-grade school.
When word got around that class was over, Satoko and Rika-chan came back into the classroom.
"...Good work today."
"Right! Time for club activities!!"
"Alright! So~ what should we do today?"
"Hey, Mii-chan. Aren't we continuing what we started yesterday?"
"Yes yes! We can't just leave it hanging like that after all the excitement — it's unsatisfying!"
"Now, now. I understand the feeling, but let's save it for another occasion! Somewhere more fitting than a classroom after school — a proper stage for it!"
"Anytime works for me. The finals are supposed to have some of the younger kids watching too, so just give me advance notice."
"Whoa! Keiichi-kun, that's amazing! You have a cheering section! A cheering section! ...I wonder if anyone's cheering for Rena..."
I caught the moment Rena slipped into her starry-eyed maiden-in-love mode.
"I won't be pulling any punches — but I'll be rooting for your victory, Rena."
Flashing a smile sweet enough to rot teeth, I ruffled Rena's hair roughly.
"Wha?! Hau~~!?!? K-K-K-K-Keiichi-kun, what... does that...hau~~..."
Rena turned scarlet and short-circuited, wisps of smoke curling from both ears.
When I peered into her eyes, I could just make out a vision of the two of us holding hands and skipping through a mysterious flower-filled meadow...
...Even knowing it was just someone else's fantasy, I found it deeply unsettling...
"...I present to you: Keiichi, Slayer of Renas."
When Rika-chan said that, everyone burst out laughing.
"Ahaha! Though that same Rena is a terror to Satoko and Rika-chan, isn't she."
"We may not be able to beat Rena, but we won't lose to Keiichi-san!!"
"Oh? Now that's an interesting dynamic. Our strengths play off each other like rock-paper-scissors! ...But that leaves Mion out entirely."
"Obviously. I don't have a weakness. I don't lose to anyone, regardless of matchups."
Mion grinned — the grin of a club president. No, the grin of an empress.
The mood in the room was heating up. The moment had come — it was time to begin!
"Alright! Let's get started!"
"Yeah! So what are we doing today, Mii-chan? Today?"
Mion made a low humming sound as she rummaged through what we called the Club Locker.
Every time I looked, that thing was packed to bursting.
Everything crammed in there was some kind of game, wasn't it?
On top of that, she also kept penalty-game props like cat ears and collars in there... the sheer capacity of that thing was beyond comprehension.
My working theory: that locker actually punched through the back wall into the fourth dimension.
Nothing else explains how she can keep pulling out one thing after another!
Mion tapped her chin in thought for a moment, then turned around without taking any game out.
"How about we keep it simple today? We've been relying on games with lots of pieces lately — it gets a bit monotonous."
"...Whatever the game is, playing together is what makes it fun."
"Yeah. That's true. That's so true!"
I nodded along with Rena. ...She's right.
With this group, even pulling weeds in the schoolyard would be a good time.
"...I wouldn't enjoy that. Pulling weeds or whatever."
"Ah, fair enough. ...That said, Satoko — stop reading people's thoughts from their expressions."
"Keiichi-kun, you wear everything on your face. Whatever you're thinking shows right away~."
I'd always been told that, but ever since I transferred here it seemed to have gotten noticeably worse...
I needed to work on a poker face or I'd be in trouble...
"Okay! Everyone pay attention~! I'll explain the rules!!"
We all stopped our idle chatter and snapped into sharp focus.
Before long everyone was listening with the watchful eyes of a tiger or a shark.
That's right. From the moment the rules are explained... the game has already begun!
"Today's game is... one that's fairly well known at parties. It's called 'Sympathy.' Has anyone heard of it?"
...Sympathy.
As in sharing someone's feelings. ...Wait, do I actually know this game?
"Mii-chan, could it be... the game where everyone picks a topic, and then writes down whatever word it makes you think of... and the more people who write the same answer, the more points you get?"
"If that's the one, Rena knows it!"
Ahh... it's coming back to me.
I'm pretty sure we played a game like that on the bus during an elementary school field trip!
"I'm not familiar with this game. Could someone explain?"
"First, the leader picks a topic. For example — say the topic is 'Dessert.'"
"Heheh! So Satoko, if someone says 'Dessert,' what's the first thing that comes to mind?"
"Hmm... let's see. Something like... 'Pudding'?"
"Got it! Then Satoko writes 'Pudding' on her notepad. Then everyone reveals their answer at the same time. If two or more people wrote 'Pudding,' those people get points!"
"The more people who match, the higher the score — if everyone writes the same answer, that's the maximum of five points!"
"...In other words, the trick is to think of the same answer as everyone else."
Exactly what Rika-chan said.
The key to this game is that thinking that one word — the same word as everyone else.
For example — you hear 'Dessert' and immediately think 'Strawberry Anmitsu!'... but that won't get you points if no one else writes the same thing.
So you have to hold back your first instinct and switch to whatever everyone else seems likely to write...
The key isn't what you yourself think — it's how well you can read what the others will answer. A deeply sophisticated battle of the mind!!
"...Mii, for Satoko's sake, let's do one practice round."
"Sure. I'll be the leader since I'm club president. The topic can be something random..."
She pulled out her Japanese literature textbook and started flipping through it.
"Alright then... just one practice round!"
"...The first topic is — 'Shaved Ice'!!!"
"Ahahaha! When you say 'shaved ice'... there's really only one answer, isn't there~!"
For shaved ice, the standards are strawberry, melon, and Blue Hawaii.
And the most popular of those is clearly melon!
"Everyone got their answers? Then reveal all at once — ready, go!"
"Strawberry." / "Melon." / "Uji-kintoki." / "Strawberry." / "Strawberry."
What the—?! I'm the only one... who wrote melon?!
"H-Hey, come on! Don't go for something so painfully ordinary like strawberry!"
"Rena! Would you actually order strawberry shaved ice?! Everyone knows melon is always the first to sell out at summer festivals!?"
"Rena loves melon too! But still... strawberry is just the classic, you know? You know~☆"
"Kei-chan, between you and me I'd actually pick melon too. But this is a word association game — if someone says 'shaved ice,' strawberry is the first thing you think of!!"
I wasn't entirely convinced... but anyway. That's the game!
"W-What does Rika's say... Is that a type of shaved ice??"
"...It's uji-kintoki. Incomparably delicious."
Sure, it's delicious, but it's a bit unusual for a first instinct... though, on reflection... that's quite a refined choice.
"Satoko, does that give you a sense of how it works? The three people who wrote 'strawberry' each get 3 points! That's how you compete!"
"Yes, yes! I understand!! More importantly, Mion-san — what will the penalty game be?!"
"Hau...! Th-There really is one... What'll it be... What'll it be!"
"Hmm... What should it be? ...Kei-chan, any suggestions?"
"Oh, the penalty game? Let me think... back in elementary school, whenever there was a penalty game... yeah. It can only be one thing."
"Bag-carrying duty!"
"So the loser has to carry everyone else's stuff home. Boys do this a lot. You see little kids staggering around under a mountain of backpacks all the time~!"
"But... how would we do that? Satoko-chan and Rika-chan's houses are in the opposite direction from ours."
"Then how about the loser just carries the winner's stuff to their house? Delivering to everyone would cover quite a distance."
"Oh, yeah. That much... sounds reasonable!"
"...Today's penalty game seems rather easy, doesn't it. At this level, it wouldn't even be satisfying to make Keiichi-san do it!!"
"Then let's raise the stakes a bit!"
"Not just carrying stuff... *ta-daaa!*"
"How about they carry everything dressed in *this*!?"
"Wh-Wha―!!!"
"A MAID OUTFIT, WHY IS IT A MAID OUTFIT!?!?"
"H-Hau...! I-It is cute, but... n-no way, no way, no way~~~~!!!!"
"...But, but! What if Keiichi-san loses?! If there's no size that fits him..."
"Not a problem~! I went ahead and had an XL prepared just for Kei-chan!!"
"So, Kei-chan, are you ready!? I'm going to parade you around outside in a full maid costume!!!"
"The headband, the stockings, everything from top to bottom — it's all right here!!"
"Th-This is insane!! Why do you have every size of that in the locker!?"
Th-This is bad, Keiichi Maebara...!!
Somehow... it's become a situation where losing has catastrophic consequences specifically for me.
What if I actually end up losing by accident?!
I'd never be able to show my face in public again!!
"Oh my, isn't that the Maebara boy?" "You heard? Apparently he has a thing for cross-dressing."
"My, really? How mortifying for his family, ohohoho!!"
"NOOOO!! Th-That's too cruel!!!!!"
That would be simply... devastating!! If I'm going to restore this penalty to something sane, now is my only chance!!!
E-Everyone, let's put a stop to this unspeakable penalty game...!!!
Why, on the walk home from school, should someone be forced to follow you around in a maid outfit carrying your bags?!
And make them call you "Master" while dragging them through the village in that embarrassing getup... *huff huff*. ...Wait, why am I huffing?
...What... is this feeling welling up inside me...
"Hey, can we just make it a regular bag-carrying penalty...!? Keiichi-kun has completely frozen up over there..."
*FWOOM*!!!
My hands, blazing with inner fire, slapped firmly down on Rena's shoulders!!!
"Rena, the problem isn't what the penalty is. ...I just have to win!! Right, everyone!!!"
I turned to face them all, my smile bright and sparkling.
"Oh! Now Kei-chan's got the right idea!!"
"Fear no penalty game!! If you win, there's nothing to worry about!! Right, everyone?! Come on, let's go—"
"YEAAAAAH!!!"
From Mion to Rika-chan, everyone thrust their fists into the air!!
Only Rena seemed unable to make up her mind, standing there flustered.
...She's a slow one. If she wins, it's quite the golden opportunity even for Rena!
"Hey, Rena. This is a seriously big chance, you know?! ...Rika-chan or Satoko-chan in a maid outfit, delivered straight to your door. The greatest opportunity in recorded history, isn't it?!"
Splurt!
Rena erupted in a magnificent nosebleed.
The color in her eyes shifted entirely... she's switched into Kyute Mode!
I've been waiting for this, Rena!! That look in your eyes!!
"Hau~~~, bring them home~~~!!! It doesn't have to be Keiichi-kun or Mii-chan either~ Hau!"
"Alright!! Everyone's ready!! Get your notepads! First question, here we go!?"
Mion flipped her textbook to a random page and read out a word that caught her eye!
"The first topic is... 'Summer Break'!!"
That's another... abstract keyword.
...Well then, I'll play it safe and go with something straightforward.
"Everyone good?! Here we go! Ready, go!!!"
At Mion's signal, everyone held up their notepads simultaneously.
Let's see... what did everyone write?
"Summer Festival." / "Summer Festival." / "Fireworks." / "Mosquito Coil."
"Oh, Rena and I matched! Alright — two points each!"
"But Satoko's 'Fireworks' was good too. And Rika-chan's 'Mosquito Coil' — it has that seasonal-word quality, like a haiku image."
"...Every answer conjures something quite cooling."
"Summer break is almost here. ...I wonder what this year's will be like."
Words that call up the cool feeling of summer... they really do lift the spirit.
Everyone seemed to have all kinds of dreams painted across their thoughts for the coming summer...
"...By the way, what did you write, Keiichi-san? Let's see it."
"Ah, ...it's... nothing worth showing..."
"No excuses! Showing it is part of the rules! Come on, come on!"
"Ah, stop it!! D-Don't look...!!"
"Homework."
Silence.
...Not a single word of sympathy from anyone.
As though my careless answer had somehow soiled the beautiful dreams of the approaching summer...
"Keiichi-kun's summers... were never fun, were they..."
"...It's alright. Let's all spend the summer together. ...We won't leave you alone, Keiichi."
Rika-chan reached up and gently patted my head.
What had summer break ever meant to me...
...Cram school.
Sunflower observation charts.
Practice exams.
Summer intensive study courses...
...I don't know anything about hot summers. I was always in an air-conditioned prep school...
...Ugh... the tears...
"...Your summers were grey, weren't they, Kei-chan..."
"W-Waaaaaaaa!!! Stop looking at me with those pitiiiiiing eeeeyes!!!"
...This game called 'Sympathy' may be more terrifying than I ever imagined...
"Anyway! Rena and I each get two points!! Keep going!! ...Next is... 'Fish'!!!"
Fish! ...Come to think of it, I haven't had sushi in a while.
For what it's worth, I happen to be a connoisseur who favors flounder over lean tuna!
...Maybe this time I'll match with someone.
There's an answer any Japanese person would think of.
"Alright, everyone! This time I'll go first!! Ready, go!!"
"Sushi."
That has to be it this time!
When you see a fresh fish, who wants to grill or steam it?
Fresh sashimi is the pinnacle!
And rather than write "sashimi," swapping in the friendlier word "sushi" — that's my cunning strategy!!
...But despite my confident answer, everyone's expressions were utterly blank.
"Oh... I see... 'Fish'... yeah. ...Sushi is tasty, isn't it."
"WaaaaAAAAA!! Don't say it in that consoling toooone!!!"
"Then everyone else reveal yours? Ready, go!"
"Aquarium." / "Dolphin." / "Whale." / "Aquarium."
"Rika and I matched! Two points!!"
"Rena went with whale? I thought about it too, but I went with dolphin."
"Oh, really?! Rena was going to write dolphin at first... but thought maybe nobody else would..."
"...Both are very kyute."
The four of them, all but me, had their thoughts drifting toward the horizon, as if they could hear the sound of waves rolling in from somewhere far away...
"So everyone has two points! Everyone except Keiichi-san!"
"...Keiichi-kun's answers were worth zero points, but... Rena thinks they were very unique and interesting! So interesting!"
"Going straight to eating when you hear the word 'fish'... Kei-chan, you should really be ashamed of how shallow your soul is."
"WaaaAAA!!"
"Don't call my soul shaaaaaallow!! Besides, what about Mion and Rena?! Those aren't even fish — they're mammals!!"
"Overruled, unanimously!! ...Right then! Next question!!"
Wh-What a rotten game this is today!!
The more rounds go by, the more I seem to be exposing myself as a shallow human being!!
I'm still the only one with zero points...!! I have to score on the next one at least!!
"Now then, the next topic is...... 'Sakura'!"
Damn it all!! I'll get it this time!!
Stay cool, Keiichi Maebara!
Don't think what you'd normally think!
Everyone else here is a girl!
Stop thinking like a man!!
If you write "Sakura Mochi" here, you'll be repeating the tragedy!
Throw out that gastrocentric male sensibility!
Just for now, think of yourself as a girl!
I'm a girl... I'm a girl... I am a girl...!!!
"Everyone done?! Here we go!!"
"Keiichi-san, won't it be dangerous if you don't score this time~?"
Hmph! This time I've got it. I've suppressed my instincts perfectly.
This time... I'll match! No — I *will* make it match!!!
"Keiichi-kun, you look so confident this time! You know, Rena thought like a boy this time to match your answer. Ehehe~... 'Sakura Mochi'☆"
"...I love them too. I wrote it."
Rena and Rika-chan giggled together.
"WhOOAAaaa!!!"
"This is not the time to be thinking about food!!"
"Then did Keiichi-san write the same as me? I put 'Hanami'!"
"That's it! When you say 'Sakura' it can only be 'Hanami,' can't it! I wrote that too!"
Mion and Satoko wrote 'Hanami'!... H-Hanami?!?!
"H-Hanami is the staple of old men getting drunk and making fools of themselves — that's what that is!!"
"And why did you suddenly give up on answering from a girl's perspective of all things!!"
"So then, Kei-chan. ...What exactly did *you* write for your girlish answer?"
"N-N-Nothing! Let's move on, next question, next— ...Ah."
Before I realized it, Rika-chan had slipped behind me and was calmly reading the note I had hidden behind my back.
"...It says 'Magical Girl.'"
...A hush fell over the room.
...Nobody said anything when the paper was revealed... or maybe nobody could?
I wished someone would at least laugh, or make fun of me, or say something dumb... but there wasn't a single reaction.
The color drained from my face, then flooded back all at once — the pressure cooker deep in my gut reached its limit and exploded.
"W-Well! 'Sakura' is the default color for magical girls, isn't it!"
"Pink hair! Pink clothes! Pink lace and frills and wands! Even I know that much!!"
"...I mean, you don't have to argue so passionately about it. You're talking about that cute show that's been on TV lately, right? Yeah, yeah, I know it."
"Yes, yes! It's so cute, isn't it?! Rena watches it every week too! It's so fun watching how the girls interact with each other~!"
"Indeed. I laugh every time the pet cat quips with its unique way of speaking!"
"That's not a cat. That's a tiger."
"When it's so small, what does it matter!"
"Anyway, that's one thing, but the fact that 'Sakura' makes you think of *that*..."
"Wh-What's wrong with it!! I was just thinking from a girl's perspective!!"
"Sakura pink equals magical girl! That's the natural way to think!! That's the only right answer!! That has to be the case!!!"
*pat*
Mion silently put her hand on my shoulder.
"Kei-chan. ...I'm certain of it now. ...You are, to the very core of your being... an old man. Past the point of correction."
"GYAAAAAAA!!"
"Why is everyone looking at me with those piiiiitiiiing eeeeyes!!"
"Th-That's not true, Keiichi-kun... OK! Let's give it our all! This is where the comeback starts!"
"An old man like Keiichi-san doesn't make comebacks!! Just quietly accept today's penalty game!!"
R-Right, today's penalty game was... what was it again?!
"...Dressing in a full maid outfit, headband and knee-socks included, while carrying the winner's belongings to their home."
"NOOOOooooOOOO!!!"
"Alright!! If you're truly members of this club, show me you can turn this around!! Let's keep rolling!!"
